---
title: "HPC End-to-End Testing"
subtitle: "Running the pipeline on Rockfish (SLURM/Singularity)"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

The `rockfish` profile targets the Johns Hopkins Rockfish cluster. These steps
cover environment setup, running the pipeline end-to-end, collecting integration
test data, and syncing results back to your local machine for test execution.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef step fill:#9b59b6,stroke:#6c3483,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    ENV["Activate nf24_env conda env"]:::step
    VCF["Generate test VCF (module load bcftools)"]:::step
    RUN["nextflow run -profile test,rockfish --legacy_assess"]:::step
    WAIT(["SLURM jobs complete"]):::data
    COLLECT["collect_test_data.sh --collect-only"]:::step
    RSYNC["rsync integration_data/ to local Mac"]:::step
    TEST["Rscript tests/run_tests.R with TEST_DB_DIR set"]:::step
    DONE(["All tests pass"]):::data

    ENV --> VCF --> RUN --> WAIT --> COLLECT --> RSYNC --> TEST --> DONE
```


## Environment Setup

```bash
conda activate /data/eande106/software/conda_envs/nf24_env
export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR=/vast/eande106/singularity
```


## Prerequisites

- Nextflow `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0` (provided by the `nf24_env` conda environment)
- SLURM account `eande106`; partition `parallel` (default) and `bigmem` (legacy
  QTL detection only)
- Singularity images cached at `/vast/eande106/singularity` (configured via
  `singularity.cacheDir` in `conf/rockfish.config`)


## Running the Pipeline on Rockfish

```bash
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv
  # vcf is specified per-group in the strainfile (see README: Strainfile Format)
```

The `rockfish` profile sets `executor = 'slurm'`, enables Singularity with
`autoMounts = true`, and applies per-process CPU/RAM/time/retry settings. Key
process resources from `conf/rockfish.config`:

| Process group | CPUs | Memory | Time | Max retries |
|--------------|------|--------|------|-------------|
| bcftools/plink extraction | 4 | 40 GB | 1 h | 3 |
| Eigen computation (`r_find_genotype_matrix_eigen`) | 2 | 60 GB | 30 min | 3 |
| GWA (simulate, GRM, map) | 4 | 20 GB | 10 min | 3 |
| DB migration (write marker set) | 2 | 8 GB | 30 min | 3 |
| DB migration (other) | 1 | 2–8 GB | 5–20 min | 2–3 |
| `r_get_gcta_intervals` (bigmem) | 1 | — | 4 h × attempt | 5 |


## Generating the Test VCF on Rockfish

Before collecting integration test data you need a local copy of the test VCF.
The generation script calls `bcftools` directly, so the tool must be on the PATH
of the subprocess — not just the interactive shell.

```bash
# 1. Check available bcftools versions and load one >= 1.16
module spider bcftools
module load bcftools/1.17   # adjust version as needed

# 2. Export PATH so the bash subprocess inherits it
#    (module load modifies the current shell's PATH but does not export it
#     automatically; without this the script exits with "bcftools not found")
export PATH

# 3. Set the full path to the WI.20210901 VCF on Rockfish
FULL_VCF=/vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20210901/vcf/WI.20210901.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# 4. Generate the test VCF (chromosomes I, II, V; 13 strains)
bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh "$FULL_VCF"
```

`data/test/test.20210901.vcf.gz` is a committed symlink that points to
`data/test/test.vcf.gz`. The script creates `test.vcf.gz` (the symlink target),
making the symlink valid for the first time on a fresh clone.


## Collecting Integration Test Data on Rockfish

Run the pipeline on the cluster, then use `--collect-only` on the login node
to gather outputs without re-running:

```bash
# 1. Run the integration test pipeline (replace <yourname> with your Rockfish username)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test

# 2. After all SLURM jobs complete, collect outputs:
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --collect-only
```

- `-work-dir` redirects intermediate files to `/scratch4/` (high-capacity scratch); without
  it Nextflow defaults to `./work/` on the login node's local disk, which is too small for
  a full pipeline run.
- Replace `<yourname>` with your Rockfish username (e.g. `ryanmckeown`).
- `--legacy_assess` is required to generate the legacy assessment file used by the
  cross-validation tests.

**Sync data to your local Mac** using `rsync` from your local terminal (not
from Rockfish). `<rockfish-host>` is whatever SSH alias or hostname you use
to connect — check `~/.ssh/config` for a `Host` entry. If you have an alias
like `Host rockfish`, use `rockfish`; otherwise use the full form
`<yourname>@login.rockfish.jhu.edu`.

```bash
# Run from your local Mac — replace <rockfish-host> and <yourname>

# Required: integration test outputs (db/, TSV assessments)
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:/vast/eande106/<yourname>/nemascan-sims-nf/tests/integration_data/ \
  tests/integration_data/

# Optional but needed for test-cross_validation.R (raw GWA files)
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:/scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test/ \
  tests/.nf-work/
```

The `--delete` flag keeps the local directory in exact sync with Rockfish
(removes any local files that no longer exist on the cluster). The trailing
`/` on both paths syncs the *contents* of the directory rather than the
directory itself.

Then run the integration tests locally:

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Note: `TEST_WORK_DIR` points to the Nextflow work directory. If you skip the
optional work-dir sync, `test-cross_validation.R` (which reads raw GWA files
from the work directory) will skip automatically — all other integration and
cross-validation tests can still run.


## Stub Runs on Rockfish

Stub runs bypass SLURM submission and verify process wiring without executing
real computations:

```bash
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv \
  -stub-run
  # vcf is specified per-group in the strainfile (see README: Strainfile Format)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## After collecting HPC data
Run the full integration test suite locally:
[Local Integration Testing](testing-integration.qmd)
:::
